summary: Soundboard source with fair tape‑speed issues; a mixed show that shines on Loser, Just A Little Light and Foolish while stumbling on Jack and Memphis.
review: The recording is a soundboard capture that suffers from noticeable speed/pitch problems – listeners report it sounds slow and needs correction, though some note moments of warm, punchy clarity. Musically the concert is uneven: early‑set songs like Loser and the debut of Just A Little Light are praised, Foolish stands out as a highlight, but several numbers (Jack, Memphis, the drum segment) fall flat, giving the overall performance a mixed vibe.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality averages around “good‑to‑average” with several standout moments but many inconsistencies; recording quality is hampered by tape‑speed issues, lowering the overall enjoyment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly mention the tape runs slow and needs pitch correction; some describe a warm, thick sound when adjusted, but overall the speed problem makes the mix sound droned and muffled. No clear audience bleed noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as slow, methodical and at times disjointed, with a few bright spots (Loser, Just A Little Light). The second set continues the uneven feel but contains a strong Foolish/Good Lovin segment. Overall the concert feels scattered and inconsistent despite occasional high points.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His lyrics are described as "ON" and his voice on Loser is highlighted; however, he also appears to be slightly out of sync with the tape speed.
band_member_comments.Phil: Briefly noted joining later in the set; no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned as possibly having had a bit too much to drink and forgetting lyrics early on, but his guitar tone later in the show is praised as "really cool".
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboards receive mixed remarks – dark, troubled songs, Hammond flourishes, synth “puppet‑string” textures, and occasional experimental sounds.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming is called "nuts" and exciting in one spot, yet the drum segment overall is criticized as semi‑ear‑splitting and foreign.
